titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 598

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=924 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.641848+00 | translation moa=925 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.650019+00

p.cl: . W . dei gracia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis Hominibus tocius terre sue Clericis et laicis  Salutem .

Scia
tis me ecclesiam Sancti chutberti de Halieland’ . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientes . et eorum
homines . et eorum possessiones uniuersas . In mea firma pace et protectione Iuste recepisse . Concessi
etiam eis ut ubique per totam terram meam liberi sint et quieti ab omni tolneio de dominicis catallis
suis . Et precipio ut ubique fugitiuos terre sue extra dominia mea inuenire poterunt 
illos Iuste habeant .

Quare prohibeo firmiter Nequis prefatis monachis . aut eorum ho
minibus iniuriam uel uiolentiam aliquam inferre presumat . aut ab eis tolneum exigat
de dominicis catallis suis . aut fugitiuos terre sue eis inuiuste detineat  Super meam plenari
am forisfacturam . Sicut Carta Regis Dauid . aui mei . Et Carta Regis . Malcolmi . fratris mei .
testantur .

Testibus . Hugone . Cancellario . meo . Æ . Abbate de Dunfermel’ . Willelmo de lindes’ . Roberto .
de londoniis . filio meo . Geruasio . auenel . Dauid . de lindes’ .
William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Know me to have lawfully received the church of Saint Cuthbert at Holy Island and the monks serving God there and their men and all their possessions under my firm peace and protection. I have also gratned to them that they be free and quit anywhere throughout my entire land from every toll on their demesne cattle. And I instruct that, wherever they can find fugitives of their land outside my demesne they may lawfully have them.

Wherefore I strictly forbid that anyone seek to cause injustice or violence to the said monks or their men, or demand toll from them for their demesne cattle, or unlawfully withhold from them fugitives of their land, on pain of my full forfeiture. As the charter of King David my grandfather, and the charter of King Malcolm my brother bears witness.
As witnesses, Hugh my chancellor, Archibald abbot of Dunfermline, William of Lindsay, Robert of London, my son, Gervase Avenel, David of Lindsay.
